titleStmt.title: SII 9.256: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 256. (A.R. No. 347 of 1905.) ON A VIRAGAL SET UP IN THE COURT-YARD OF THE TALAKANTAMMA TEMPLE AT DEVAGUDI, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is damaged and dated the 13th year of the Chāḷukya king Jagadēkamalla (II), cyclic year Śukla, Pausha, amāvāsye, Ādivāra corresponding probably to A.D. 1149 December 31, Saturday, not Sunday. It states that, when Kaṭakada-Chandra-Daṇḍanāyaka's brother-in-law Madhuvarasa invaded the country of Kuṟumaṟi with 40 horses, a servant of Mallōja, the Heggaḍe of the Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bhīmarasa of Kuṟumaṟi fell in a fight with Madhuvarasa, having slain a cavalier and a horse.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmat sukula saṁvatsara
jaganēkamallakāla hadimūṟaneyapauṣada
Amāvāsyeyādivāra samastaprasastisahitaṁ śrīmanma
mahāmaṁḍaḷēsvaraṁ kuṟumaṟiya bhīmarasana hegghaḍeya
mallōjana baṁṭa belapemāma kuṟumaṟiya hasuṁpada
holamēre sīmakke kaṭakada caṁdradaṁḍanāyakana
maidunaṁ madhuvarasaṁ nālvattu kudureya berasi baṁdu
holamēreyalu kāḷagadiṟidaḍe tannāḻdaṁge
harigeyaṁnikki baṁ
du kudureyaṁ
rāhutanaṁ
koṁdu svarggha
stanāda
Digital edition of SII 9.256 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
